name: googlesheets-automation
description: Automate Google Sheets operations (read, write, format, filter, manage spreadsheets) via Rube MCP (Composio). Read/write data, manage tabs, apply formatting, and search rows programmatically.
capabilities: Read data from Google Sheets spreadsheets using batch operations
Write and update cell values, ranges, or append new rows
Create new spreadsheets and add or manage worksheet tabs
Search and filter rows by exact cell values or criteria
Apply cell formatting including bold, colors, and font sizes
Upsert rows based on unique key columns for data synchronization
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 139 static findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' detections are Markdown code spans (backticks) used for formatting tool names, not shell execution. The 'hardcoded URL' is the official Rube MCP server endpoint in documentation. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code - it provides instructions for using Google Sheets via the Rube MCP service.
valueStatement: Stop manually updating spreadsheets. This skill helps you read, write, format, and manage Google Sheets programmatically through Claude using Composio integration.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and Google OAuth authentication
Rate limited to 60 reads and 60 writes per minute by Google Sheets API
Cannot execute code or perform operations outside of Google Sheets scope
Depends on external Rube MCP service availability and network connectivity

useCases.title: CRM Data Synchronization
useCases.description: Keep customer records synchronized between your database and Google Sheets. Use upsert operations to update existing contacts or add new ones based on email addresses as unique keys.

useCases.title: Inventory Tracking
useCases.description: Maintain real-time inventory levels in shared spreadsheets. Read current stock, update quantities after sales, and format low-stock items with red background colors for visibility.

useCases.title: Report Generation Automation
useCases.description: Generate weekly reports by pulling data from multiple sheets, formatting headers with bold styling, and writing summary metrics to a dashboard sheet for team review.

promptTemplates.title: Read Sheet Data
promptTemplates.prompt: Read the data from 'Sheet1' in my Google Sheet named 'Q4 Sales Data'. Show me the first 100 rows from columns A through F.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner users wanting to view spreadsheet contents

promptTemplates.title: Update Multiple Cells
promptTemplates.prompt: Update the 'Inventory' spreadsheet. In the 'Stock' sheet, set quantity to 50 for rows where SKU is 'PROD-001' through 'PROD-010'. Use batch update for efficiency.
promptTemplates.scenario: Updating specific rows with known values

promptTemplates.title: Sync with Database
promptTemplates.prompt: Upsert these customer records into the 'Clients' sheet in my 'CRM Database' spreadsheet. Use 'Email' as the key column to update existing contacts or add new ones: [your data here]

promptTemplates.title: Format and Dashboard
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new sheet called 'Summary' in my 'Analytics' spreadsheet. Copy the total row from 'Data' sheet, make the headers bold with blue background, and set column widths to 200.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to verify connection and get current tool schemas before operations
Use bounded ranges like 'A1:Z1000' instead of unbounded ranges like 'A:Z' to avoid timeouts on large sheets
Batch multiple read operations together with GOOGLESHEETS_BATCH_GET to stay within the 60 reads/minute rate limit
Read existing sheet data before writing to understand the current layout and avoid overwriting important information
antiPatterns: Do not use unbounded ranges (e.g., 'A:Z') on sheets with more than 10,000 rows as this causes timeouts
Do not assume default sheet names like 'Sheet1' exist - they are locale-dependent and may differ in non-English accounts
Do not perform more than 60 read or write operations per minute or Google Sheets API will return rate limit errors

faq.question: Do I need a Google API key?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses the Rube MCP service which handles Google OAuth authentication. You only need to add the Rube MCP server endpoint and authenticate through the provided OAuth link.

faq.question: What are the rate limits?
faq.answer: Google Sheets allows 60 reads and 60 writes per minute. Exceeding these limits causes errors. Use batch operations (BATCH_GET, BATCH_UPDATE) to combine multiple operations and stay within limits.

faq.question: Can I work with multiple sheets at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Use GET_SHEET_NAMES to list all tabs, then reference each by name in your operations. You can read from multiple sheets in one BATCH_GET call.

faq.question: Why does my sheet tab name fail?
faq.answer: Sheet names are case-insensitive but must match exactly. Default names are locale-dependent (e.g., 'Sheet1' in English, 'Hoja 1' in Spanish). Use GET_SHEET_NAMES to verify actual tab names.

faq.question: How do I format cells with colors?
faq.answer: Use FORMAT_CELL with the numeric worksheet_id (not tab name) from GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO. Color values are floats from 0.0 to 1.0, not 0-255 integers. For red background, set red=1.0, green=0.0, blue=0.0.

faq.question: What is the difference between update and upsert?
faq.answer: UPDATE overwrites cells at a specific location. UPSERT updates existing rows that match a key column (like Email) or appends new rows if no match is found. Use UPSERT for synchronization scenarios where you want to avoid duplicates.
